free_text: Study of activation cross sections of proton induced
reactions on barium: Production of 131Ba->131Cs
Main reference.
Preliminary data.
Preliminary data in: Abstracts (p.118) of
6th Int. Conf. on Isotopes (6ICI),
Seoul, Korea, 12-16 May 2008.
AVF-930 cyclotron.
Thin barium chloride dihydrate tablets (BaCl2*2H2O,
99.9%, 40-80 mg/cm2, from Sigma-Aldrich) were
prepared by pressing powdered material in special cups
made of aluminum (370 um bottom thickness)
and covering them with Al foil (10 um).
The stacked targets were bombarded with proton beam,
low beam intensity: 25 nA,
beam energy: 70 MeV,
irradiation time: 20 min.
Irradiation under helium gas atmosphere in a water
cooled target holder.
The radioactivity of each sample (total cup with Ba as
target material of interest and Al cover and backing
serving as monitors) was measured nondestructively.
The data acquisition was started only about 20 h after
the end of bombardment.
High purity Al backing, bottom thickness: 370 um,
cover thickness: 10 um.
Chapter 4
Volume 3
The energy degradation along the stack was calculated
on the basis of this polynomial approximation.
The decay data of the contributing reactions were
taken from NuDat2.5 database.
The energy uncertainties were determined
taking into account the uncertainties of primary
energy and foil thicknesses and the energy straggling.
The uncertainties were calculated in the
standard way: the uncertainties of the linearly
contributing independent parameters were quadraticaly
summed and its square root was taken.
By authors: data point at 37.7 MeV shows a systematic
deviation from expected values inferred from the mean
trend of the excitation function, probably due to a
non-homogenous distribution of target material
or a broken pellet.
Comparison with experimental results found
in the literature.
Comparison with new results of the ALICE-IPPE
and EMPIRE-II codes and with existing data obtained
with TALYS code (EAF-2007).
From the measured cross section data integral
yields were calculated.
KB
Data were taken from Table 2 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
The cross section was measured after the total decay
of the metastable state (IT 76.0%) to the ground state.
Data were taken from Table 2 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
As 135La decays exclusively into the ground state of
135Ba, independent cross sections of 135mBa were
measured.
Data were taken from Table 3 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
The rather short lived 133La decays partly to the
ground state and partly (1.6%) to the metastable state
of 133Ba. As the 133mBa activity could be measured for
the first time after nearly total decay of 133La, the
cross section is cumulative.
Data were taken from Table 3 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
This cumulative cross section contains full
contribution from the decay of 133La and also 133mBa
because it was measured after their nearly total decay.
Data were taken from Table 3 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
This cumulative cross section was measured after
complete decay of parent 131La and metastable 131mBa.
Data were taken from Table 3 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
136Cs is a closed radioisotope, i.e. its neighbors
with the same mass number are stable.
This cross section contains contribution of the
metastable state after its total decay to the
ground state.
Data were taken from Table 4 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
134Cs is a closed radioisotope and the cross
section was measured after total decay of
the metastable state to the ground state.
Data were taken from Table 4 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
Data were taken from Table 4 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
This cumulative cross section involves the contribution
of 129La -> 129g,mBa -> 129Cs decay chain in addition
to the production of 129Cs.
Data were taken from Table 4 of the Ref.:
Applied Radiation and Isotopes 68 (2010) 1869.
